---
title: "Risk Mapping & Disease Surveillance"
---

::: {.section-intro}
# Risk Mapping & Disease Surveillance

This work asks where schistosomiasis transmission systems and transmission-relevant organisms are likely to occur, and where surveillance can be most informative. These projects operate at broad spatial scales, from landscapes to national surveillance priorities.
:::

::: {.project-card-grid}

::: {.project-card}
![Schistosomiasis and snail host modeling image](images/schisto-snail-modeling.jpg){.project-card-image}

### Schistosomiasis and snail host modeling

Using host ecology, spatial models, and uncertainty analysis to improve predictions of schistosomiasis risk and guide survey design.

**Lead research · disease ecology · spatial modeling · uncertainty**

[See more →](projects/schisto-snail-modeling/){.see-more}
:::

::: {.project-card}
![Crayfish range mapping image](images/crayfish-range-mapping.jpg){.project-card-image}

### Crayfish range mapping and surveillance priorities

Modeling the potential distribution of invasive crayfish and identifying where spread may overlap with schistosomiasis transmission landscapes.

**Lead research · invasive species · surveillance · spatial prediction**

[See more →](projects/crayfish-range-mapping/){.see-more}
:::

:::
